task:
Full dashboard overhaul — all three batches in one run. PM can plan up to 3 stages.

TARGET ARCHITECTURE (approved by user):

DATA MODEL:
- pipelines[] own workspaceId (drop workspace.pipelineIds arrays)
- ownership map unifies claims + assignments: { claimedBy[], assignedTo? }
- SubtaskKey branded type with make(stageRef,id)/parse(key) — replaces stringly-typed :: everywhere
- archived: { stages[], pipelines[], subtasks[] } — no trash
- NO lockedPipelines, no trashedStages/Pipelines/Subtasks, no isLocked()
- stageId becomes immutable slug/uuid; stageNameOverrides survives for display

STATE LAYERS:
- useSync: network — poll-with-since, patch, optimistic via useOptimisticMutation hook
- useModel: domain — ownership, reactions, comments, subtasks
- <ModelContext>: read-only views + handlers (kills 30+ prop drilling)
- <EphemeralContext>: reactOpen/commentOpen/copied/hover (UI state only)
- Single sync state machine: hydrating → live ↔ offline → error

EDIT MODE (replaces lock):
- Per-card editMode boolean. Pencil click → toggle.
- Card gets t.accent+"08" bg tint while in edit mode
- Edit mode is the ONLY gate to: rename, archive, desc edit, priority/color, subtask reorder/remove
- Default shows: claim/react/comment/assign + drag only
- Escape or click-outside exits edit mode

VISUAL LANGUAGE:
- Pipeline color: left edge accent + icon ONLY (never card border)
- Claimed: pipeline.color+"55" border tint
- Status: t.cyan/amber/green/red — pill + column header only
- Warning: t.amber ONLY — pending/blocked/unknown-parent
- Captain: gold-on-bg (👑 + bold), NEVER amber pill
- Edit mode active: t.accent+"08" bg on whole card

TYPOGRAPHY RULE: mono = counts/IDs/status keys/points; sans = names/titles/descriptions. Two sizes max per component: primary 13, meta 10/11. NO 9pt 800-weight micro-CTAs.

MOTION: Three durations only — 150ms (hover/click), 300ms (expand/collapse via max-height NOT fadeIn), 1400ms (celebration). ONE claimPulse at a time.

VOICE: lowercase, terse, irreverent. "mine" not "claimed". "take" for subtasks. "// connecting to command..." not "Loading". "// empty waters" not "no tasks".

CARD VOCABULARY:
- Stage cards: "claim" / "mine"
- Subtask cards: "take" / "mine"
- Single <ClaimChip> component: two variants (claimed/unclaimed). Replaces all three current treatments.
- Single status pill: always rendered, always clickable (discovery), drag = fast path.

COMPONENT TREE TARGET:
- Dashboard.tsx: routing + sync only (~200 lines from current ~2100)
- ChromeShell: Sidebar + Header (ThemePicker, NotificationBell, UserMenu)
- Routes: HomeView, PipelinesView, KanbanView, ChatView, ActivityView, ArchiveView
- ModalRoot: workspace modals

SERVER CONTRACT:
- GET /api/state?since=<updatedAt> → {patch?, updatedAt} (304 if unchanged)
- PATCH /api/state: recursive whitelist + deep key validation (no $, ., __proto__)
- POST /api/state/comments|reactions: session-authoritative by field
- POST /api/state/activity: server-authored only (ignore body.user)
- No lockedPipelines anywhere

BATCH A (dead code + correctness):
- Remove isLocked, lockedPipelines everywhere — client handlers, server route, validate.ts, UI
- Remove trashedStages/trashedPipelines/trashedSubtasks — archive only
- Server: recursive nested-key validation in PATCH
- Server: session-authoritative activity attribution (ignore body.user)
- Fix Stage.tsx:249 — onBlur calls setStageDescOverride instead of setStageNameOverride; convert to controlled input

BATCH B (design polish):
- Pencil = only gate to edit mode; whole card gets tint frame in edit mode
- Single <ClaimChip> component, "mine" verb everywhere; "take" for subtasks
- Pipeline color → left edge + icon ONLY (remove from card border unless claimed)
- Amber → warning ONLY; captain badge → 👑 + bold, no amber tint
- Loading spinner → "// connecting to command..." typewriter animation
- Stage card transition: standardize to 150ms/300ms/1400ms (remove 0.4s/0.2s mix)
- Stage expand: max-height slideDown instead of instant fadeIn
- One claimPulse at a time (only most-actionable claim pulses)
- Empty states in voice: "// empty waters", "// nothing here yet" etc.

BATCH C (architecture):
- useSync hook: delta-poll with ?since=, single state machine hydrating→live↔offline→error
- useOptimisticMutation(slice,pushFn) hook used by addComment/handleReact/handleClaim
- ModelContext + EphemeralContext: kill prop drilling
- pipelines own workspaceId; migration from workspace.pipelineIds arrays
- SubtaskKey branded type with make/parse helpers
- ownership map unifies claims+assignments
- Dashboard.tsx → ~200 lines (extract PipelinesView, KanbanView, ChatView, ActivityView, ArchiveView)
- Server: GET /api/state?since= with 304 short-circuit

stages.spec:
## Stage 1 — Batch A: dead code removal + correctness

### Why this will be good
This stage hardens the foundation by ripping out stale lock/trash code paths and closing two real correctness bugs (Stage.tsx onBlur misnaming, server activity attribution spoofing). After this stage, the codebase has fewer moving parts, the server is session-authoritative for who-did-what, and Batch B can layer visual polish onto a clean substrate without inheriting any locked-pipeline branches.

Production-ready checklist:
- Existing claim/comment/react flows still work (no regression on the happy path)
- 423 PIPELINE_LOCKED responses no longer exist; the routes return the same shape they always did for unlocked pipelines
- localStorage migration is silent: existing users with `lockedPipelines` / `trashedStages` keys load without crashing
- TypeScript strict mode passes

### Files to touch

**1. `src/lib/PipelineState.ts`** (Mongoose schema)
- Remove the `lockedPipelines: { type: Mixed }` field from the schema. Keep the rest intact.
- Do NOT add a Mongo migration script — the field will be ignored on read (Mongoose drops unknown fields) and stripped on next save. Document this in a one-line comment above the schema.

**2. `src/lib/validate.ts`**
- Remove `lockedPipelines` from the `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS` whitelist if present.
- Remove any `trashedStages`/`trashedPipelines`/`trashedSubtasks` keys from the whitelist.
- Add a NEW exported helper `validateNestedKeys(obj: unknown, depth = 0): boolean` that walks any nested object passed to PATCH and rejects keys containing `$`, `.`, `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`. Max depth 6. Return false if invalid; the route returns 400 on false.
- Keep `validateStageKey()` and `validatePatchKeys()` exactly as they are.

**3. `src/app/api/pipeline-state/route.ts`** (PATCH + GET)
- Remove all 423 PIPELINE_LOCKED branches. Remove `lockedPipelines` from any whitelist or special-case handling.
- In PATCH, after `validatePatchKeys`, also call `validateNestedKeys(body)` recursively. Return 400 with `{ error: "INVALID_KEY" }` on failure.
- Keep `status_change` activity emission as-is.

**4. `src/app/api/pipeline-state/activity/route.ts`**
- Remove the 423 PIPELINE_LOCKED branch.
- **Server-authoritative attribution:** ignore `body.user` entirely. Read `session.user.fixedUserId` from `getServerSession(authOptions)` and use that as the authored user. The activity item written to Mongo and emitted to chatBus must use this server value. If no session, return 401 — do not silently fall through.

**5. `src/app/api/pipeline-state/comments/route.ts`**
- Remove the 423 PIPELINE_LOCKED branch.
- Same server-authoritative attribution: comment.user = session.user.fixedUserId, ignore body.user.

**6. `src/components/Stage.tsx`** — surgical fix at the onBlur
- Find the input around line 249 whose `onBlur` currently calls `setStageNameOverride` but is bound to the description value. Repoint that `onBlur` to `setStageDescOverride`.
- Convert the description input to a CONTROLLED input: `value={stageDescOverride[stage] ?? stageDefaults[stage]?.desc ?? ""}` and `onChange={(e) => setLocalDesc(e.target.value)}` with local state, persisting to override on blur. Mirror the existing pattern used for the name input above it.
- No visual change — this is correctness only.

**7. `src/components/Dashboard.tsx`** — strip lock state plumbing
- Remove `pipelineLocks` state (`useState<Record<string, boolean>>`).
- Remove `isLocked(pipelineId)` and `isStageInLockedPipeline(stageName)` helpers.
- Remove every call site that gates an action on `isLocked` — the action becomes unconditional.
- Remove the `lockedPipelines` field from any `patchState({...})` call.
- Remove `trashedStages`/`trashedPipelines`/`trashedSubtasks` if any remain (the data model spec says they’re gone; verify and excise).
- Remove the `isLocked` prop being passed into `Stage.tsx` and any other child. Remove the prop from those children’s typed prop interfaces.
- localStorage cleanup: in `clearAllLsKeys` flow it’s already wiped; for users on old schema, simply ignore stale `binayah_lockedPipelines` if it exists (do NOT crash if read returns unexpected shape).

**8. `src/lib/storage.ts`** (only if it explicitly references lock keys)
- Remove any `LS_KEYS` entries for `lockedPipelines` / trashed-* keys if they exist as named constants.

**9. Schema version bump** (`src/lib/version.ts`)
- Bump `SCHEMA_VERSION` from current value to next (e.g. `"v2"` → `"v3"`). This forces a one-time `clearAllLsKeys()` for existing users on next mount, eliminating any straggling `binayah_lockedPipelines` / `binayah_trashedStages` keys cleanly. Add a one-line comment: `// v3 — dropped lockedPipelines + trashedStages`.

### Out of scope (DO NOT do in Stage 1)
- Do not touch Stage.tsx visuals beyond the line 249 fix
- Do not introduce edit mode, ClaimChip, or any Batch B copy/visual change
- Do not extract any view component out of Dashboard.tsx
- Do not add useSync, useModel, or contexts
- Do not change the GET /api/pipeline-state contract (no `?since=` yet)

### Acceptance criteria
- `grep -rn "isLocked\|lockedPipelines\|PIPELINE_LOCKED\|trashedStages\|trashedPipelines\|trashedSubtasks" src/` returns zero matches (excluding archived state filenames if any)
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with no new errors
- The dashboard loads, a stage can be claimed, status can be changed, a comment can be posted — all without 423 responses
- Activity entries written via the API show the session user, even if a malicious client posts `{ user: "someone-else" }`
- Stage.tsx description override now persists on blur (it currently doesn’t — it overwrites the name field)

### What QA verifies
1. Open the dashboard. Pick any stage. Claim it. Status pill changes through concept→planned→in-progress→active. No 423 toast appears.
2. Open Stage.tsx description (expanded card), edit text, click outside. Re-open card. Description persists; stage name is unchanged.
3. DevTools network tab — PATCH /api/pipeline-state never returns 423. Activity POST does not include `lockedPipelines` in the payload.
4. localStorage inspection: no `binayah_lockedPipelines` or `binayah_trashedStages` keys after first reload (schema version bump cleared them).
5. Reviewer check: search the diff for the strings `isLocked`, `lockedPipelines`, `423`, `PIPELINE_LOCKED`, `trashed` — must be zero hits in non-test source files.

## Stage 2 — Batch B: design polish (visual language landing on a clean substrate)

### Why this will be good
Stage 1 cleared dead code; Stage 2 makes the dashboard feel like a single coherent surface. One claim chip everywhere, one status pill everywhere, one motion vocabulary, one voice. Pipeline color demoted to a left-edge accent stops fighting the status color for attention. Edit mode replacing the lock model gives the user direct visual feedback for what they can mutate. The card stops being a wall of micro-CTAs.

The success test: a designer who has never seen the app should be able to look at one stage card and tell, in <2s, who owns it, what status it’s in, and whether it’s currently being edited.

Production-ready checklist:
- Empty states in voice: "// empty waters", "// nothing here yet", "// connecting to command..."
- Edit mode is reversible (Escape, click-outside)
- One claimPulse at a time — no jitter when multiple stages just transitioned
- Mobile (<768px) still readable; tap targets clear 44×44
- Auth state unchanged (login still works)

### Files to touch

**1. `src/components/ui/ClaimChip.tsx`** — NEW component
- Single chip component with two variants: `claimed` (current user is in claimers) and `unclaimed`.
- Props: `{ t: Theme; claimed: boolean; pipelineColor: string; verb?: "claim" | "take"; onClick: () => void; pulse?: boolean }`.
- Default verb is `claim` (stage cards). Subtask cards pass `verb="take"`. Both variants use "mine" when claimed.
- Visual: pill, 13px primary text (sans), 150ms hover. Unclaimed = `t.border` outline + `t.textMuted` text. Claimed = `pipelineColor + "22"` bg + `pipelineColor + "55"` border + `t.text` foreground (NEVER amber). Pulse uses one shared `claimPulse` keyframe at 1400ms when `pulse=true`.
- DEV decides exact padding/radius — reference the existing claim button pattern in DESIGN_SYSTEM.md §Component patterns and pick the cleanest version.
- Replaces ALL three current claim treatments across Stage.tsx, KanbanView.tsx, and any subtask UI.

**2. `src/components/ui/StatusPill.tsx`** — NEW component (extract from existing inline status rendering)
- One pill rendered always, always clickable. Click cycles through `STATUS_ORDER`.
- Drag is preserved as the fast path (existing kanban drag behavior unchanged).
- Props: `{ t: Theme; status: Status; onClick: () => void; size?: "sm" | "md" }`.
- Color from canonical map: concept→textMuted, planned→amber, in-progress→cyan, active→green. Mono font for the status keyword (typography rule: status keys = mono).
- Replaces the current inline pill rendering in Stage.tsx and KanbanView.tsx column headers.

**3. `src/components/Stage.tsx`** — the card overhaul
- **Edit mode state:** `stageEditMode` boolean already exists as local state in Stage.tsx (added pre-Stage-1). Do NOT add a duplicate — wire the existing `stageEditMode` / `setStageEditMode` to the pencil and click-outside logic below.
- **Pencil = sole entry to edit mode:** the existing pencil/edit affordance is the only toggle. Click → set editMode=true. Click again or press Escape or click outside the card → set editMode=false. Use a `useEffect` with a document-level listener for click-outside; ref the card root.
- **Edit-mode visual:** when `editMode=true`, the whole card root gets `background: t.bgCard` blended with `t.accent + "08"` overlay. Smooth 150ms transition.
- **Edit mode is the ONLY gate** to: rename, archive, description edit, priority/color cycle, subtask reorder/remove. Everything else (claim, react, comment, assign, drag) remains available always.
- **Pipeline color demoted:** remove `border: pipeline.color` on the card. Render a `4px` left-edge accent bar (or padding-left + box-shadow inset) using the pipeline color, plus the pipeline icon in pipeline color. The card border itself uses `t.border` by default. When the current user is a claimer, the border becomes `pipeline.color + "55"` (this is the only case the pipeline color hits the border).
- **Captain badge:** find the captain treatment (currently amber pill). Replace with `👑` + bold name, color `t.amber` ONLY in the icon if needed for gold-on-bg legibility — but the badge container must NOT use amber bg/border. NEVER an amber pill.
- **Amber lockdown:** every other amber usage in Stage.tsx is reviewed. Amber survives ONLY for warnings (pending/blocked/unknown-parent) and the planned status. Audit and replace any decorative amber with `t.accent` or `t.cyan` as appropriate.
- **Replace claim treatments** with `<ClaimChip>`. Replace inline status rendering with `<StatusPill>`.
- **Expand/collapse motion:** the current "instant fadeIn" on stage expand becomes a `max-height` slideDown over 300ms. Use CSS transition `max-height 300ms ease, opacity 150ms ease`. Set `max-height: 0` collapsed, `max-height: 2000px` (generous upper bound) expanded. DEV picks the bound that doesn’t clip subtask lists at scale.
- **No 9pt 800-weight micro-CTAs.** Audit every `fontSize: 9` or `fontWeight: 800` on text inside the card. Bring to 10/11pt 600-weight max. Counts/IDs/points stay mono per typography rule.
- **Subtask cards** use `<ClaimChip verb="take">`.
- **Voice copy:** wherever "claimed" appears in user-visible text (not localStorage keys, not API field names), change to "mine".

**4. `src/components/KanbanView.tsx`**
- Use `<StatusPill>` in column headers (preserves status color canon).
- Use `<ClaimChip>` on stage tiles.
- Empty column copy: `// empty waters` (mono, t.textMuted).
- Pipeline color: tile gets left-edge accent only, not full border. Same rule as Stage.tsx.

**5. `src/components/Dashboard.tsx`** (motion + loading polish only — no architectural change yet)
- **Loading state:** the current spinner becomes a typewriter typing `// connecting to command...` over ~600ms. Use a `useEffect` that ticks character-by-character. Mono font, t.textDim. After the 4-state machine lands in Stage 3 this becomes the `hydrating` state copy.
- **claimPulse coordination:** the existing `claimAnim` state in Dashboard already carries `{ stage, pts }`. Make it singleton-aware: when a new pulse fires while one is active, the new one wins (replaces) rather than queuing. Pass `pulse` prop into ClaimChip only for the most-actionable claim (the one currently in claimAnim).
- **Motion durations standardized:** sweep Dashboard.tsx for any `transition: ... 0.4s` / `0.2s` / `0.5s` / `200ms` etc. Force into the 150 / 300 / 1400 vocabulary. 150ms for hover/click, 300ms for expand/collapse, 1400ms for celebration only.

**6. Empty states audit** (across Dashboard.tsx, KanbanView.tsx, ActivityFeed.tsx, ChatPanel.tsx, OverviewPanel.tsx, DocumentsPanel.tsx)
- Replace "No tasks", "No activity yet", "No messages", "No documents" with voice variants:
  - Tasks/stages: `// empty waters`
  - Activity: `// nothing here yet`
  - Chat: `// silence on the wire`
  - Docs: `// no scrolls yet`
- Mono font, `t.textDim`, lowercase, no period.

### Out of scope (DO NOT do in Stage 2)
- Do not extract Dashboard view components yet (Stage 3)
- Do not introduce ModelContext, EphemeralContext, useSync, useModel, useOptimisticMutation
- Do not change the data model (no SubtaskKey, no ownership map, no workspaceId migration)
- Do not change the GET /api/pipeline-state contract
- Do not add new endpoints

### Acceptance criteria
- One `ClaimChip` component used everywhere; old claim button code paths in Stage.tsx and KanbanView.tsx are removed.
- One `StatusPill` component used everywhere status is rendered; old inline pill code is removed.
- `grep -rn "claimed" src/components` returns only programmatic identifiers (state field names, function names) — no user-visible "claimed" copy.
- Card border in default state uses `t.border`; pipeline color appears only as left-edge accent + icon (and as `+"55"` border when current user is a claimer).
- No amber pills outside warning/planned-status contexts. Captain badge is `👑 + bold name`, no amber container.
- Pencil click toggles edit mode; `t.accent + "08"` tint visible; click outside or Escape exits.
- Stage expand uses max-height 300ms transition (no instant fadeIn).
- Loading state types `// connecting to command...` instead of showing a spinner.
- All empty states use voice copy in mono.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

### What QA verifies
1. Visual sweep across all 4 themes (warroom, lab, engine, nerve) in dark + light — 8 combinations. Stage card looks coherent in each. No amber pill on a non-warning surface.
2. Click pencil on a stage → card tints → click outside → tint clears. Press Escape → tint clears. Drag a stage in kanban while NOT in edit mode — still works. Try to rename a stage outside edit mode — input is not present/editable.
3. Claim a stage. The chip changes. Only ONE claimPulse animation fires even if you claim two stages within 500ms. The most-recent one is the one pulsing.
4. Expand a stage with subtasks. Motion is smooth slide, not instant.
5. Empty pipeline shows `// empty waters` in mono. Empty chat shows `// silence on the wire`. Disconnect Wi-Fi briefly — reconnect shows the typewriter `// connecting to command...`.
6. Mobile <768px: card readable, tap targets clear 44×44. ClaimChip fits the column.
7. Reviewer search the diff for forbidden patterns: `fontSize: 9`, `fontWeight: 800` (on inline-styled CTA text), `transition: ... 0.4s`, `transition: ... 0.2s` — either gone or justified.

## Stage 3a — Batch C Part 1: context extraction + Dashboard split

### Why this will be good
Dashboard.tsx is 2068 lines. This stage extracts pure-UI state into EphemeralContext, all model state + handlers into ModelContext, and splits the render tree into named view components. After this stage, Stage.tsx and KanbanView.tsx no longer receive 30+ props; Dashboard.tsx routes between views. The data-model changes (SubtaskKey, ownership, workspaceId) land in Stage 3b on a clean foundation.

The success test: `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400 after Step 3.3 completes (we accept up to 400 here; Stage 3b will finish the reduction to ~200 once hooks take over the last polling block).

### Extraction order (DO IN THIS ORDER — commit after each step)

#### Step 3.1 — EphemeralContext (lowest risk — UI-only state move)

**File:** `src/lib/contexts/EphemeralContext.tsx` — NEW

Provider state:
- `reactOpen: string|null`, `setReactOpen`
- `commentOpen: string|null`, `setCommentOpen`
- `copied: string|null`, `setCopied`
- `hoverStage: string|null`, `setHoverStage`
- `editModeStage: string|null`, `setEditModeStage`
- `modalState: { type: string; payload?: unknown } | null`, `setModalState` (for ModalRoot in Step 3.3)

These are pure UI flicker state. No persistence, no API calls, no theming.

Hook: `useEphemeral()` returns the full bag. Components destructure what they need.

**Migration:** sweep Dashboard.tsx for the matching `useState`s, remove from Dashboard, wrap the dashboard tree in `<EphemeralProvider>`, and convert call sites in Stage.tsx, KanbanView.tsx, etc. to `useEphemeral()`.

**Done when:** Stage.tsx no longer accepts `reactOpen` / `setReactOpen` / similar as props. Dashboard.tsx is shorter by these `useState`s. Existing UI behavior unchanged.

#### Step 3.2 — ModelContext (read views + handlers)

**File:** `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` — NEW

Provider value:
```ts
{
  pipelines, stages, reactions, comments, subtasks,
  statusOverrides, descOverrides, nameOverrides, users, currentUser,
  theme: t,
  syncStatus,   // "connecting" | "live" | "offline" — from Dashboard's existing useState
  handlers: {
    handleClaim, handleReact, handleStatusChange, addComment,
    addSubtask, toggleSubtask, lockSubtask, removeSubtask,
    archiveStage, archivePipeline, setStageDescOverride, setStageNameOverride,
    // ... all other handlers Dashboard currently defines
  }
}
```

ALL handlers Dashboard currently defines move INTO the provider. The provider owns model state in this step (still using existing `lsGet/lsSet` + `patchState`; these are replaced by useSync in Stage 3b).

Specialized selectors: `useStage(stageId)`, `useOwnership(stageId)`, `usePipeline(pipelineId)` — memoized via `useMemo` per slice.

Hook: `useModel()` returns the full value.

**Migration:** Stage.tsx and KanbanView.tsx stop receiving claims/reactions/comments/subtasks/handlers as props. They pull from `useModel()` / `useStage(id)`.

**Done when:** Stage.tsx prop interface drops from ~30 props to ~5 (`stageId`, `pipelineId`, `t`, `idx`, `expS`/expand callback). Dashboard.tsx no longer threads handler functions through children.

**NOTE on existing sync state:**
- `isInitializedRef` (useRef, line ~372 in Dashboard.tsx): keep in ModelContext provider, it guards the effect that avoids writing stale localStorage over API on mount. It does NOT move to useSync — that happens in Stage 3b.
- `isHydrating` (useState, line ~383): keep in Dashboard.tsx for now; Stage 3b's useSync replaces it.
- `syncStatus` ("connecting"|"live"|"offline", useState, line ~382): move into ModelContext value so views can read it. The polling setInterval stays in Dashboard until Stage 3b.

#### Step 3.3 — Dashboard split into route components

**New files in `src/components/views/`:**
- `PipelinesView.tsx` — pipeline-list rendering currently in Dashboard.tsx
- `ChatView.tsx` — wraps the existing ChatPanel as a routed view
- `ActivityView.tsx` — wraps ActivityFeed as a routed view
- `ArchiveView.tsx` — NEW — renders `archivedStages[]`, `archivedPipelines[]`, `archivedSubtasks[]` from ModelContext. Each item shows what it was, when archived, with an "unarchive" affordance. If no archived data, show `// nothing archived yet`.

**Existing views (already split out — verify they consume ModelContext):**
- `src/components/HomeView.tsx` — already exists. Refactor to consume `useModel()` for any props currently threaded from Dashboard.
- `src/components/TasksView.tsx` — already exists. Same.
- `src/components/KanbanView.tsx` — already exists. Same.

**`src/components/ChromeShell.tsx`** — NEW
- Renders LeftSidebar + top header bar. Header contains ThemePicker (extract from Dashboard), NotificationBell, UserMenu (extract avatar/sign-out cluster from Dashboard).

**`src/components/ModalRoot.tsx`** — NEW
- Reads `modalState` from `useEphemeral()`. Renders WelcomeModal, WorkspaceAdmin modal based on `modalState.type`.

**`src/components/Dashboard.tsx`** — reduced
- Wraps `<EphemeralProvider><ModelProvider><ChromeShell><RouteSwitch/><ModalRoot/></ChromeShell></ModelProvider></EphemeralProvider>`.
- `RouteSwitch` is a tiny switch on `activeNavItem` rendering the appropriate `*View`.
- The polling `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block stays here for now (Stage 3b replaces it).
- `isHydrating` stays here for now (Stage 3b replaces it).
- Target after this step: ≤ 400 lines. If over 500, stop and escalate before proceeding to Stage 3b.

**Done when:** `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400. Each `*View.tsx` is independently readable. No view component imports another view.

### Out of scope (save for Stage 3b)
- Do NOT implement useOptimisticMutation, useSync, SubtaskKey, ownership map, or workspaceId migration.
- Do NOT rename or create `/api/state` route.
- Do NOT add `archived: { stages, pipelines, subtasks }` shape to PipelineState — ArchiveView in this stage just reads the existing `archivedStages[]` arrays.

### Acceptance criteria
- `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400.
- `grep -rn "useState" src/components/Dashboard.tsx | wc -l` ≤ 15 (only routing, sync, hydrating state remain).
- `src/lib/contexts/EphemeralContext.tsx` and `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` exist and are consumed.
- Stage.tsx prop count ≤ 8 (excluding implicit theme via ModelContext).
- No `reactOpen` / `setReactOpen` / `commentOpen` / `setCommentOpen` props on Stage.tsx.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. `npm run build` succeeds.
- Existing user flows: claim, react, comment, drag, status change, chat, docs, search all work end-to-end.

### What QA verifies
1. Dashboard loads. `// connecting to command...` typewriter shows, then state hydrates. syncStatus dot turns green.
2. Claim a stage — ClaimChip updates. React — reaction updates. Comment — comment appears.
3. Archive a stage — ArchiveView shows it. Unarchive — it returns.
4. Mobile: sidebar hidden, BottomSheet panels functional.
5. `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400.
6. No TypeScript errors.

### Notes for DEV
- Commit after each step (3.1, 3.2, 3.3). Three commits. Each one is independently revertable.
- If Step 3.2 leaves Dashboard.tsx over 600 lines, the ModelContext migration is incomplete — do NOT proceed to 3.3 until all handler threads are gone from Dashboard.
- After Stage 3a ships, update `.claude/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`: add entries for EphemeralContext and ModelContext under §0 codebase map.

bugs.description: Stage.tsx Props interface not slimmed — still ~28 props including claims, reactions, comments, handleClaim, handleReact, addComment, addSubtask and all handlers. Spec requires ~5 props after ModelContext migration.
bugs.reproSteps: Run: sed -n "126,170p" src/components/Stage.tsx — observe full Props interface still contains all model state and handler props
bugs.expected: Stage.tsx Props interface to contain only ~5 props: stageId, pipelineId, t, idx, expS/expand callback (per spec Step 3.2 Done criteria)
bugs.actual: Stage.tsx Props interface still has ~28 props; Stage.tsx does NOT call useModel(), only useEphemeral() for reactOpen/copied. PipelinesView.tsx still passes all model data down to Stage as props.

bugs.description: Specialized selectors useStage(), useOwnership(), usePipeline() not implemented in ModelContext.tsx — spec required memoized per-slice selectors
bugs.reproSteps: Run: grep -n "useStage\|useOwnership\|usePipeline" src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx — returns zero results
bugs.expected: Exported useStage(stageId), useOwnership(stageId), usePipeline(pipelineId) selectors in ModelContext.tsx
bugs.actual: No specialized selectors exist; only useModel() returns full context bag

qaReport.designSkipReason: structural refactor only — no visual/design changes in this stage

passing_checks: View files exist: ActivityView, ArchiveView, ChatView, HomeViewRoute, PipelinesView, TeamBar
ModelContext.tsx poll/sync wired: 7 matches for setInterval/fetchState/patchState
HomeView.tsx: uses useModel() at line 8+39; 0 props.* references
No runtime errors in dev server log
notes.partial_credit: EphemeralContext migration complete. ModelContext created and all view files migrated. Stage.tsx partial: only useEphemeral() wired, useModel() migration not done. Dashboard.tsx now routes to named view components as required.

stages.spec:
## Stage 3b — Batch C Part 2: hooks + data-model migration

### Why this will be good
Stage 3a left Dashboard.tsx routing over named views with context providers. This stage installs the final pieces: useOptimisticMutation turns all writes into one-liner rollback-safe operations; useSync replaces the hand-rolled polling loop with a typed state machine; SubtaskKey makes stringly-typed `::` patterns unrepresentable; ownership map unifies claims; workspaceId migration removes the `pipelineIds` array inversion. After this stage, Dashboard.tsx is ~200 lines and the state machine is explicit.

The success test: `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250. `setInterval` no longer appears in Dashboard. Network tab shows mostly 304 responses after initial load.

### Execution order (commit after each step)

#### Step 3.4 — useOptimisticMutation hook

**File:** `src/lib/hooks/useOptimisticMutation.ts` — NEW

Signature:
```ts
useOptimisticMutation<TSlice, TArgs>(
  applyLocal: (slice: TSlice, args: TArgs) => TSlice,
  push: (args: TArgs) => Promise<void>
) => (args: TArgs) => Promise<void>
```

Behavior:
- Applies `applyLocal` immediately to local state via the appropriate ModelContext setter.
- Awaits `push`. On reject: rollback (re-apply prior slice) + show toast `// sync failed — retrying`.
- Internal queue: if `push` is in-flight, subsequent calls coalesce (last-write-wins on same slice key) rather than queue serially.

**Migration:** convert `addComment`, `handleReact`, `handleClaim` inside ModelContext to use this hook. Each handler body becomes 1–3 lines.

**Done when:** exactly one rollback path per mutation type; the three handlers above are 1–3 lines each.

#### Step 3.5 — useSync hook + state machine + delta-sync server route

**File:** `src/lib/hooks/useSync.ts` — NEW

State machine (replaces `isHydrating` + `syncStatus` + `isInitializedRef` in Dashboard/ModelContext):
```
"hydrating" → (first successful GET) → "live"
"live" ↔ (poll fail) → "offline" → (success) → "live"
"live" | "offline" → (4xx) → "error" (toast, stays until reload)
```

**Explicitly replaces these variables (NOTE: Stage 3a moved all of these from Dashboard into ModelContext.tsx):**
- `isHydrating` (useState in Dashboard.tsx line ~126) → `status === "hydrating"` from useSync (Dashboard drops this useState)
- `syncStatus` ("connecting"|"live"|"offline", ModelContext.tsx line ~200; exposed on context value; Dashboard reads it from context) → `status` returned by useSync, passed through ModelContext value
- `isInitializedRef` (useRef in ModelContext.tsx line ~203 — already removed from Dashboard by Stage 3a) → internal to useSync
- The `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block (ModelContext.tsx line ~400 — already removed from Dashboard by Stage 3a) → useSync's internal polling loop

Polling behavior:
- `live`: polls `GET /api/pipeline-state?since=<lastUpdatedAt>` every 8s (±1s jitter)
- `offline`: retries every 2s, backoff capped at 30s on repeated failure
- Returns `{ status, lastSyncAt, manualSync }`

The `// connecting to command...` typewriter (already in Dashboard from Stage 2) maps to `status === "hydrating"`.

**Server-side delta sync — extend existing GET `/api/pipeline-state`:**
- Add `?since=<ISO>` query param support.
- If `since` is provided and `state.updatedAt <= since`: return `304 Not Modified` (empty body).
- Otherwise return `{ patch: <state>, updatedAt }` as JSON.
- Verify `timestamps: true` is set on PipelineState schema (Mongoose auto-updates `updatedAt` on save). If missing, add it.
- Preserve existing GET (no `?since=`) shape — no breaking change.
- Do NOT create a new `/api/state` route — extend `/api/pipeline-state`.

**Migration in ModelContext/Dashboard:**
- Call `useSync(fetchState, patchState)` inside ModelContext provider (the polling hook belongs here alongside the data). Pass its `status` on ModelContext value (replacing the current `syncStatus` field).
- Remove `syncStatus` useState + `isInitializedRef` useRef + `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block from ModelContext.tsx.
- Remove `isHydrating` useState from Dashboard.tsx (already derives from `syncStatus` from context — update to `status === "hydrating"`).
- `isInitializedRef` is already gone from Dashboard.tsx (Stage 3a moved it) — no action needed there.

**Done when:** ModelContext.tsx has no `setInterval(fetchState...)`, no `syncStatus` useState, no `isInitializedRef` useRef. Dashboard.tsx has no `isHydrating` useState. Network tab shows 304s after initial load.

#### Step 3.6 — SubtaskKey branded type + ownership map + workspaceId migration (LAST)

**SubtaskKey branded type — `src/lib/types/SubtaskKey.ts` (NEW):**
```ts
type SubtaskKey = string & { readonly __brand: "SubtaskKey" }
namespace SubtaskKey {
  function make(stageRef: string, id: string): SubtaskKey  // "${stageRef}::${id}", validates no "::" in either part
  function parse(key: SubtaskKey): { stageRef: string; id: string }
}
```
The `::` format stays as the serialized MongoDB/localStorage form. Only CLIENT-SIDE creation and parsing are typed. Replace every `${stage}::${subtaskId}` string literal in business logic with `SubtaskKey.make(...)`. TypeScript strict mode catches remaining raw strings.

**Ownership map — replace `claims`:**
- New shape: `ownership: Record<string, { claimedBy: string[]; assignedTo?: string }>` (key is stageRef).
- Replaces both `claims: Record<string, string[]>` AND `assignments: Record<string, string>` everywhere in ModelContext state.
- NOTE: Stage 3a already added `useOwnership(stageId)` selector in ModelContext (line ~761) that returns `{ claimers, isMine, assignedTo, isAssignedToMe }`. Update this selector to read from the new `ownership` map — the selector's return shape stays identical, so consumers are unaffected.
- `handleClaim` and `assignTask` handlers write to `ownership` map; external API (function signatures) unchanged.
- Migration on hydrate (one-time in ModelContext): if old `claims` shape present, fold into `ownership` (set `claimedBy = claims[stage]`, `assignedTo = assignments[stage]`), write back via PATCH, clear `claims` and `assignments`.
- Server (`validate.ts`): add `ownership` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS` whitelist. Keep `claims` and `assignments` in whitelist for the migration write-back.

**pipelines own workspaceId — 3-step migration:**
- (a) Add `workspaceId?: string` field to `Pipeline` type in `src/lib/data.ts`. Static pipeline definitions do NOT need a hardcoded value — the migration sets it at runtime.
- (b) Migration on hydrate (one-time in ModelContext): for each workspace in `workspaces[]`, read `workspace.pipelineIds[]`, and for each id, find the matching pipeline and set `pipeline.workspaceId = workspace.id` in state. If a pipeline has no `pipelineIds` entry (legacy/missing), assign it to the singleton default workspace. Write updated pipelines back via PATCH (`pipelineWorkspaceMap: Record<string, string>` — a lightweight id→workspaceId map stored in PipelineState, NOT the full pipeline objects). Add `pipelineWorkspaceMap` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS`.
- (c) After migration: use `pipelines.filter(p => p.workspaceId === wsId)` instead of `workspace.pipelineIds`. Drop `pipelineIds` from the `Workspace` type in `data.ts` only after the migration helper confirms all pipelines have a workspaceId.

**Archive map — structured shape for ArchiveView:**
- New PipelineState shape: `archived: { stages: ArchivedItem[]; pipelines: ArchivedItem[]; subtasks: ArchivedItem[] }`.
- `ArchivedItem = { id: string; archivedAt: number; archivedBy: string; payload: unknown }`.
- Migration on hydrate: fold existing `archivedStages: string[]` into `archived.stages[]` (set `id`, `archivedAt = 0`, `archivedBy = ""`, `payload = null` for legacy items). Same for archivedPipelines, archivedSubtasks.
- ArchiveView (from Stage 3a) now reads `archived.*` instead of flat arrays.
- Add `archived` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS`.

**Done when:** no raw `${stage}::${id}` string in business logic; `claims` replaced by `ownership`; `workspace.pipelineIds` gone from Workspace type; `archived` is a structured map; `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.

### Out of scope
- Do not introduce Redux/Zustand/Jotai.
- Do not rename `/api/pipeline-state`.
- Do not migrate ChatPanel's local state into ModelContext.

### Acceptance criteria
- `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.
- `grep -rn "useState" src/components/Dashboard.tsx | wc -l` ≤ 5 (routing-level only).
- `useSync` is the only polling primitive. No `setInterval(fetchState...)` in Dashboard or ModelContext.
- `isHydrating` useState gone from Dashboard.tsx. `syncStatus` useState + `isInitializedRef` useRef gone from ModelContext.tsx.
- `useOptimisticMutation` used by addComment, handleReact, handleClaim. Each handler 1–3 lines.
- `SubtaskKey.make()` replaces all raw `::` concatenations in business logic.
- `ownership` replaces `claims`; migration is silent on fresh load with old data.
- `pipelines[].workspaceId` set at runtime; `workspace.pipelineIds` absent from Workspace type.
- `archived` is structured map; ArchiveView reads from it.
- `GET /api/pipeline-state?since=<ISO>` returns 304 when state is unchanged.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. `npm run build` succeeds.
- Existing user flows end-to-end: claim, react, comment, drag, status, chat, docs, search.

### What QA verifies
1. Cold load: `// connecting to command...` shows, then live. DevTools state machine in `hydrating` → `live`.
2. Network tab: most polls are 304. Second-tab change → next poll returns 200, UI updates.
3. Kill network → `offline` indicator. Restore → `live` within one poll cycle.
4. Claim offline → optimistic apply. Reconnect → push succeeds. Force 500 → rollback toast.
5. Subtask reorder in edit mode. SubtaskKey paths handle it. localStorage + Mongo agree on reload.
6. Stale localStorage with old `claims` + `workspace.pipelineIds` → after mount, both gone; reload silent.
7. `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.
8. `grep -n "setInterval" src/components/Dashboard.tsx src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` → no match in either file.
9. PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §3 updated with ownership shape, workspaceId, archived map.
10. PROJECT_CONTEXT.md has useSync state machine + `?since=` contract documented.

### Notes for DEV
- Commit after each step (3.4, 3.5, 3.6). Three commits.
- Step 3.6 migration helpers must be idempotent — running them twice must produce the same result.
- After Step 3.5, update `.claude/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`: add state machine states + `?since=` contract.
- After Step 3.6, update PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §3: ownership shape, `pipelines[].workspaceId`, `archived` map.

plannerOutput: Full requirements embedded in task field. PM: plan up to 3 stages. Batch A = dead code/correctness, Batch B = design polish, Batch C = architecture. PM decides how to split based on risk/testability.

- Call `useSync(fetchState, patchState)` inside ModelContext provider (the polling hook belongs here alongside the data). Pass its `status` on ModelContext value (replacing the current `syncStatus` field).
- Remove `syncStatus` useState + `isInitializedRef` useRef + `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block from ModelContext.tsx.
- Remove `isHydrating` useState from Dashboard.tsx (already derives from `syncStatus` from context — update to `status === "hydrating"`).
- `isInitializedRef` is already gone from Dashboard.tsx (Stage 3a moved it) — no action needed there.

**Done when:** ModelContext.tsx has no `setInterval(fetchState...)`, no `syncStatus` useState, no `isInitializedRef` useRef. Dashboard.tsx has no `isHydrating` useState. Network tab shows 304s after initial load.

#### Step 3.6 — SubtaskKey branded type + ownership map + workspaceId migration (LAST)

**SubtaskKey branded type — `src/lib/types/SubtaskKey.ts` (NEW):**
```ts
type SubtaskKey = string & { readonly __brand: "SubtaskKey" }
namespace SubtaskKey {
  function make(stageRef: string, id: string): SubtaskKey  // "${stageRef}::${id}", validates no "::" in either part
  function parse(key: SubtaskKey): { stageRef: string; id: string }

```
The `::` format stays as the serialized MongoDB/localStorage form. Only CLIENT-SIDE creation and parsing are typed. Replace every `${stage}::${subtaskId}` string literal in business logic with `SubtaskKey.make(...)`. TypeScript strict mode catches remaining raw strings.

**Ownership map — replace `claims`:**
- New shape: `ownership: Record<string, { claimedBy: string[]; assignedTo?: string }>` (key is stageRef).
- Replaces both `claims: Record<string, string[]>` AND `assignments: Record<string, string>` everywhere in ModelContext state.
- NOTE: Stage 3a already added `useOwnership(stageId)` selector in ModelContext (line ~761) that returns `{ claimers, isMine, assignedTo, isAssignedToMe }`. Update this selector to read from the new `ownership` map — the selector's return shape stays identical, so consumers are unaffected.

**Archive map — structured shape for ArchiveView:**
- New PipelineState shape: `archived: { stages: ArchivedItem[]; pipelines: ArchivedItem[]; subtasks: ArchivedItem[] }`.
- `ArchivedItem = { id: string; archivedAt: number; archivedBy: string; payload: unknown }`.

- ArchiveView (from Stage 3a) now reads `archived.*` instead of flat arrays.
- Add `archived` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS`.

**Done when:** no raw `${stage}::${id}` string in business logic; `claims` replaced by `ownership`; `workspace.pipelineIds` gone from Workspace type; `archived` is a structured map; `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.

### Out of scope
- Do not introduce Redux/Zustand/Jotai.
- Do not rename `/api/pipeline-state`.
- Do not migrate ChatPanel's local state into ModelContext.

### Acceptance criteria
- `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.
- `grep -rn "useState" src/components/Dashboard.tsx | wc -l` ≤ 5 (routing-level only).
- `useSync` is the only polling primitive. No `setInterval(fetchState...)` in Dashboard or ModelContext.

- `archived` is structured map; ArchiveView reads from it.
- `GET /api/pipeline-state?since=<ISO>` returns 304 when state is unchanged.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. `npm run build` succeeds.
- Existing user flows end-to-end: claim, react, comment, drag, status, chat, docs, search.

### What QA verifies
1. Cold load: `// connecting to command...` shows, then live. DevTools state machine in `hydrating` → `live`.
2. Network tab: most polls are 304. Second-tab change → next poll returns 200, UI updates.

4. Claim offline → optimistic apply. Reconnect → push succeeds. Force 500 → rollback toast.
